title: Use metaprogramming sparingly and test it
description: Reserve method_missing/define_method for real need and pin the dynamic behavior with tests since tools cannot see it.
body: Use when tempted to generate methods dynamically. Do not reach for method_missing when an explicit method or a simple loop of define_method would do, and never add method_missing without a matching respond_to_missing?. Actions: prefer explicit methods; if dynamic, implement both method_missing and respond_to_missing? so introspection and duck typing work, and cover each generated method with a test. Validation signals include specs that call the dynamically defined methods and assert respond_to? returns true. Risk boundary: undocumented metaprogramming defeats grep, static analysis, and IDE navigation, hiding behavior from reviewers; an unbounded method_missing that silently absorbs unknown calls masks typos as silent no-ops.
